output.question: Fetch https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon and extract his birth date, death date, and three key contributions to information theory.
output.findings:
Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and he passed away on February 24, 2001.

His foundational work in information theory includes several key contributions. First, he established the concept of the 'bit' as the fundamental unit of information. Second, he formulated the noisy-channel coding theorem, which defines the theoretical maximum rate at which information can be transmitted over a communication channel with a given noise level. Third, he introduced the concept of entropy as a measure of the uncertainty or information content of a random variable, which became a cornerstone of modern communication systems.

output.final_answer: Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and passed away on February 24, 2001. Three of his key contributions to information theory include: 1) Establishing the 'bit' as the fundamental unit of information; 2) Formulating the noisy-channel coding theorem, which determines the maximum rate of error-free data transmission; and 3) Introducing the concept of entropy as a measure of uncertainty or information content. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon
